name: Shipping Container Office
description:
Shipping Container Office — 20-ft and 40-ft, Purpose-Built for Office Use

Buyers typing "shipping container office" are usually at one of two decision points — choosing between the 20-ft and 40-ft form factor, or confirming whether the unit they are about to receive is a purpose-built office structure or a retrofitted used cargo container. Both decisions affect what you get on site, what documentation comes with it, and how long it lasts.

Shipping Container Office — Form Factor, Not Just a Container with a Door

SAMAN 40-ft shipping container office deployed at a national highway construction project — IS 2062 frame, electrical-fitted, single-unit installation.

A SAMAN shipping container office is a new ISO-grade Corten steel structure fabricated to shipping container form factor — corrugated steel shell, welded perimeter frame, ISO corner castings at four corners, and forklift pockets at the base. It is not a second-hand cargo container with an office cut into it.

The Indian market also lists retrofitted used containers under similar search terms. Those units carry no traceable material certification and typically lose internal height to add-on flooring — different from a purpose-built unit. The procurement-grade decision (new vs refurbished, when each makes sense) is covered on our container office hub page; this page focuses on the form-factor decision in front of buyers using the search term itself. If your requirement is a lighter cabin-form workspace under a 6-month project horizon, a portable office cabin is usually the right fit instead.

What a 40-ft Shipping Container Office Adds Over a 20-ft — Truck, Crane, and Customizations

SAMAN 20-ft and 40-ft units shown side by side — visual size comparison for buyers choosing the right form factor.

The two standard form factors are 20-ft (5,500mm × 2,200mm internal floor) and 40-ft (11,800mm × 2,200mm). Internal width and ceiling height are identical at 2,400mm. Length is what changes — along with the logistics around it.

A 20-ft unit fits a single flat-bed truck without overwidth permits and lifts with a standard 25-ton crane. A loaded 40-ft unit needs a 40-ton telescopic crane minimum and tighter site-access planning — vehicle approach within 20 metres of the placement position, an eight-metre crane swing radius clear of overhead obstructions.

Customisation budget also goes further on the 40-ft shell — partition walls, additional windows, AC zones, and network conduit cost less per square metre across a longer floor. For team-fit logic at the site-office level — engineers per unit, drawing review zones — see our construction site office page.

The "ISO" in Shipping Container Office — Why It Matters in Procurement

SAMAN unit at a bridge construction project — ISO 1161 corner castings visible, ready for crane lift to next site phase.

"ISO" on a shipping container office means three specific things in procurement terms.

First — the four corner castings at top and bottom are dimensioned to international standards, which is what makes the unit crane-liftable from any standard spreader bar and stacking-rated for 2-level configurations. Second — the structural specification is documented (IS 2062 Corten steel grade, 3mm minimum wall thickness), which government tenders, port operators, and inspection-required projects ask for at the order stage. Third — the unit qualifies for GST-compliant invoicing as a fabricated office structure with a material test certificate attached.

A retrofitted used container offers none of these in writing. Stacked configurations have their own engineering and are covered separately.

What's Built In, What's an Add-On — Standard vs Custom Fabrication

SAMAN 40-ft unit at a thermal power plant project — standard fitout including AC bracket, distribution box, and grilled windows.

Every SAMAN shipping container office ships with the same standard interior baseline. 50mm PUF insulated panels on all six faces — wall, ceiling, and floor — maintaining an 8 to 10°C differential against outside temperature. 240V single-phase electrical wiring with switchboard, MCB panel, and lighting fixtures. One lockable steel personnel door. Window count and glazing per the standard fabrication drawing.

What is not standard: AC unit (only the provision point is pre-wired), partition walls, additional windows, network conduit, generator socket, document alcove, custom paint colour, exterior branding. These are confirmed at the order stage and fabricated before dispatch — they are not site-installable add-ons. For the full order-stage decision process, see our prefabricated container office page.

Lead Time and Single-Unit Delivery — From Order to Erected on Site

SAMAN 40-ft unit just delivered to a warehouse project — typical 7 to 21 working day lead time from Bangalore or Greater Noida.

Single-unit lead time from confirmed order is 7 to 14 working days for fabrication, plus 3 to 10 working days transit depending on destination state — total 7 to 21 working days for a single unit dispatched from our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility.

Ground requirement for a single unit is four levelling pads on compacted ground — no concrete slab needed. For stacked configurations, multi-container office complexes, or scaled-up offices built from connected units, see our modular shipping container office page — that is a different engineering scope.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does "shipping container" refer to in this product name?

It refers to the structural form factor — ISO container dimensions (20-ft × 8-ft × 8-ft 6-in, or 40-ft × 8-ft × 8-ft 6-in external), four ISO corner castings rated for crane lifting and 2-level stacking, and a Corten steel corrugated shell welded to a perimeter frame. The "shipping container" in the name describes the build standard, not the use — a SAMAN unit is fabricated to that standard purpose-built for office use, not a cargo container converted later.

Does a standard SAMAN shipping container office come with an AC, or only the provision for one?

Provision only. The standard fitout includes 50mm PUF insulation on all six faces, 240V single-phase wiring with switchboard, MCB panel, and lighting fixtures — plus a wired and sleeved AC provision point ready to accept a split AC unit. The AC unit itself is added at order stage as a customisation or supplied separately by the buyer. PUF insulation maintains an 8 to 10°C differential against outside temperature, which keeps the unit functional without AC in milder conditions and reduces AC load when one is fitted.

What is the price difference between a 20-ft and a 40-ft shipping container office?

A 40-ft unit costs approximately 60 to 80 percent more than a comparable 20-ft unit at standard fitout — the price gap reflects double the floor area against a single set of corner castings, doors, and electrical baseline. Custom fitout (additional windows, partitions, AC zones) widens the gap. We share unit-specific pricing within 24 hours once we know team size, site location, and customisation requirements.

Get a Specification and Price for Your Shipping Container Office

Tell us your team size, site location, and required delivery date — or call us and we will confirm the right unit and lead time in under 10 minutes.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937 Manufactured in Bangalore and Greater Noida. Delivered and erected Pan India in 7–21 working days.
short_description:
ISO-grade Corten steel · Purpose-built for office use
20-ft & 40-ft · Pan India in 7–21 days
Shipping container office in two standard form factors — 20-ft and 40-ft — fabricated from new IS 2062 Corten steel at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities. Stacking-rated, crane-liftable, electrical-fitted, AC-ready.
Size
20×8 ft / 40×8 ft
Capacity
4–6 / 8–12 people
Frame
IS 2062 Corten
Panel
50mm PUF panel
Price
Quote-based
Delivery
7–21 working days
FloorLoad
500 kg / sq m
Relocations
8–10 crane cycles
Bangalore + Greater Noida
Call +91 97089 89937 →
